diagrams.title: OS Position in Computing Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A layered diagram showing the operating system as an intermediary layer between computer hardware and user applications. This visualizes the OS definition by showing its position in the system stack.
elements: Top layer: User Applications (web browser, word processor, games)
Middle layer: Operating System (kernel, system services, user interface)
Bottom layer: Computer Hardware (CPU, memory, storage, input/output devices)
Arrows showing interaction flow between layers
diagrams.purpose: Explains the definition of an OS as system software that manages hardware resources and provides services to application programs, acting as an interface between users and computer hardware.

diagrams.title: OS Core Components Structure
diagrams.description: A structural diagram showing the main components and functions of an operating system in a unified view. This helps understand how the OS is structured internally.
elements: Kernel (process scheduler, memory manager, file system, device drivers)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the internal structure of an OS, showing the kernel at the core with its essential functions for process management, memory management, file system, and I/O operations, surrounded by system programs and user interfaces.

diagrams.title: OS Purpose and Functions
diagrams.description: A conceptual diagram illustrating the five primary purposes of an operating system using a central hub with connected function boxes.
elements: Process Management (CPU scheduling, process control, thread management)
File Management (create, delete, organize files and directories)
Device Management (device drivers, I/O handling, buffer management)
Security and Protection (user authentication, access control, permissions)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the five fundamental purposes of an OS: to manage system resources efficiently, provide user interface, enable program execution, handle file operations, and ensure system security and protection.
